item.title: Open Mic to amplify public voices for women’s and girls’ rights
item.description: As part of global campaign to mark the International Women’s Day, the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) in Bangladesh listened to common people in an Open Mic event at Shahabuddin Park in Gulshan on Wednesday. The Open Mic event forms part of UNFPA Bangladesh’s broader Internation

item.title: US says Iran firing fewer missiles, US strikes to expand inland
item.description: Iran is firing fewer missiles than it did at ‌the start of the war on Saturday, the top U.S. general said on Wednesday, as he argued Iran’s ​military capabilities were greatly diminished as the ​United States expands its strikes inside Iran. “We will ⁠now begin to expand inland st

item.title: Govt moving to restore discipline in public hospitals: Health minister
item.description: Health and Family Welfare Minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Hossain on Wednesday said the government will take firm and effective measures to stop irregularities in public hospitals and restore discipline in the healthcare system. He made the remarks during a surprise visit to Dhaka Medical College Hosp

item.title: IFIC Bank signs MoU with Cumilla City Corporation
item.description: IFIC Bank PLC has entered a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Cumilla City Corporation with a view to enhancing citizen services, improving efficiency in service delivery, and extending modern banking services to the doorstep of the people, according to a press release. The MoU was signed on

item.title: Canada's services PMI shows activity declining further in February
item.description: Canada's services economy contracted for a fourth straight month in February as an uncertain trading environment contributed to declines in ​activity and new business, S&P Global's Canada services PMI data ‌showed on Wednesday. The headline Business Activity Index edged up to 46.5 last month

item.title: Action if waste collection fee exceeds Tk 100, DSCC Administrator
item.description: Dhaka South City Corporation (DSCC) Administrator Abdus Salam on Wednesday warned that action will be taken against contractors if they charge more than the fixed Tk 100 fee for door-to-door household waste collection. He made the remarks while addressing a special cleanliness and mosquito control

item.title: IFIC Bank launches series of exclusive offers for its credit, debit cardholders to mark Ramadan
item.description: In celebration of the holy month of Ramadan and the upcoming Eid-ul-Fitr, IFIC Bank has launched a series of exclusive offers for its credit and debit cardholders. According to a press release, the bank has introduced extensive cashback and discount facilities across various categories, including

item.title: Bank Asia observes Financial Literacy Day-2026
item.description: Bank Asia PLC observed ‘Financial Literacy Day–2026’ on March 2, 2026, by organising a series of initiatives in line with the directives of Bangladesh Bank, according to a press release. To mark the occasion, the bank arranged a nationwide virtual meeting with its officials. Mana

item.title: Education minister vows tighter monitoring to boost quality of primary education
item.description: Education Minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Wednesday announced closer monitoring and enhanced oversight to improve the quality of primary education, saying necessary steps will be taken to accelerate work and motivate all concerned. “Education begins at the primary school level. So we mus

item.title: ‘Internet blackout killings’: First witness against Joy, Palak testifies
item.description: The first witness has completed testimony and cross-examination in a crimes against humanity case against Sajeeb Wazed Joy, son and ICT advisor to ousted prime minister Sheikh Hasina and former state minister for ICT Zunaid Ahmed Palak. The pair are accused of orchestrating an internet shutdown to

item.title: Nasiruddin Patwary voices concern over Sylhet roads, urges quick repairs
item.description: Chief Coordinator of the National Citizen Party (NCP) Nasiruddin Patwary has expressed dissatisfaction over the poor condition of several roads in Sylhet, warning that public suffering will worsen if urgent repairs are not undertaken. He made the remarks while addressing an iftar mahfil organised

item.title: Khosru returns home after hospital stay
item.description: After four days of treatment, Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury has returned home from Gulshan’s United Hospital. On Wednesday, his personal assistant Shahiduzzaman said doctors advised the minister to rest, though he continues urgent official work from home. “He managed c
